name: Mary Martin's Popcorn Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Keep the popped corn hot and crisp in a slow oven set to 300°F.
2. In a large saucepan, combine sugar, corn syrup, water, salt, vinegar, and vanilla. Cook over low heat until the mixture reaches 254°F to 260°F.
3. Continue cooking until the temperature reaches 264°F. Add food coloring if desired.
4. Slowly pour the hot syrup mixture over the popped popcorn, stirring well to ensure each kernel is coated.
5. Shape the popcorn into balls and let cool until firm.
6. Wrap in plastic wrap.
Mary was a spinster neighbor when I was a child who had to be the youngest woman I have ever known and the best substitute grandmother a child could have ever wished for. She knew that her mother had been making this recipe as far back as 1880.
